_meta.description: Adversarial test cases for the domain knowledge engine. Every case in must_fail MUST be blocked or flagged. Every case in should_pass MUST be approved.

must_fail.name: Eliminate all safety stock
must_fail.recommendation: We should eliminate all safety stock across our warehouse network to reduce holding costs and free up working capital. This will save approximately 15% on inventory carrying costs.
must_fail.reason: Eliminating all safety stock during seasonal demand with elevated disruptions is indefensible. Must BLOCK.

must_fail.name: Single source all critical components
must_fail.recommendation: To reduce procurement complexity and get better pricing, we should consolidate all critical component sourcing to our preferred supplier, SupplierX. This will reduce PO processing costs by 30%.
must_fail.reason: Single-sourcing critical components is a CRITICAL supply chain risk regardless of disruption environment.

must_fail.name: Forecast improvement claim without data
must_fail.recommendation: By implementing our new AI demand forecasting platform, we will improve forecast accuracy from 60% to 95% within 3 months, allowing us to cut safety stock by 40% immediately.
must_fail.reason: 95% accuracy claim in 3 months is unrealistic. Reactive maturity cannot support advanced ML. Must flag.

must_fail.name: JIT in volatile environment
must_fail.recommendation: We should implement just-in-time inventory management across our entire supply chain to achieve zero inventory and maximum capital efficiency.
must_fail.reason: JIT in a volatile environment with lumpy demand is a high-risk combination. Must BLOCK or CONDITIONAL.

must_fail.name: Extend payment terms to 180 days
must_fail.recommendation: To improve our working capital position, we will extend supplier payment terms from 30 days to 180 days across our entire supplier base, starting immediately.
must_fail.reason: 180-day DPO will cause SME supplier bankruptcies and supply disruption. Must flag as CRITICAL risk.

must_fail.name: No IT integration plan
must_fail.recommendation: We will connect our ERP, WMS, TMS, and supplier portals into a real-time data platform to provide end-to-end supply chain visibility across 50 suppliers within 6 months.
must_fail.reason: ERP+WMS+TMS+50 suppliers in 6 months is massively underestimated. Must flag.

must_fail.name: Cost-only optimisation ignoring resilience
must_fail.recommendation: Cut all supply chain costs by 25% through supplier consolidation, inventory reduction, and shifting to sea freight for all shipments including time-sensitive components.
must_fail.reason: Blanket 25% cost cut with no resilience guardrails in elevated disruption environment. Must flag multiple risks.

should_pass.name: Dual-source critical supplier
should_pass.recommendation: Qualify a second source for our top 3 critical components, maintaining the primary supplier for 70% of volume and the secondary for 30%. This reduces single-source risk while leveraging existing supplier relationships.
should_pass.reason: Textbook resilience improvement. Should PASS with no critical challenges.

should_pass.name: Gradual safety stock reduction with lead time data
should_pass.recommendation: After measuring supplier lead time CV (currently at 8%), we propose a 15% phased reduction in safety stock over 6 months, with rollback triggers if fill rate drops below 96%.
should_pass.reason: Data-backed, phased, with explicit rollback criteria. Should PASS.

should_pass.name: S&OP process improvement with executive sponsorship
should_pass.recommendation: Implement a monthly S&OP process with executive sponsorship from the COO. Phase 1 covers Sales and Operations alignment on 13-week demand signal. Phase 2 extends to supplier collaboration in month 4.
should_pass.reason: Well-scoped, phased, with named sponsorship. Should PASS.
